title: Extracellular Enzyme Assays for NAG, BG, and CBH
author: Mia Maltz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jg3cjyn

steps.title: Weigh other part (1-2 g wet...
steps.instruction: Weigh other part (1-2 g wet weight), record mass, and place in labeled 500 ml container.

steps.title: Add 125 ml acetate buffer and...
steps.instruction: Add 125 ml acetate buffer and blend on highest speed for 1 minute to make a homogenate slurry. undetermined, acetate buffer 00:01:00, blending on highest speed

steps.title: Rinse blender with DI water between samples
steps.instruction: Rinse blender with DI water between samples.

steps.title: a Using the multi-channel pipette with wide-mouth tips (i
steps.instruction: a Using the multi-channel pipette with wide-mouth tips (i.e., trimmed with scissors), add the homogenate to all wells receiving homogenate

steps.title: a All wells should now contain 250 μl
steps.instruction: a All wells should now contain 250 μl. Place plates in the incubator at a proper temperature for soils (e.g., 10°C for Alaskan soils). Incubate for one hour. 01:00:00, incubation

steps.title: After one hour of incubation, add...
steps.instruction: After one hour of incubation, add 10 μL of 1 M NaOH to each well, noting time. This is not done in advance. Add the NaOH to the plates and wait 10 min, then read the plates. This is important. Give the NaOH time to interact with the MUB. MUB will only fluoresce in alkaline conditions. undetermined, 1 M NaOH

steps.title: Read fluorescence plates at 365 nm...
steps.instruction: Read fluorescence plates at 365 nm excitation and 450 nm emission.

steps.title: It is important to calculate the...
steps.instruction: It is important to calculate the standard curve for the MUB based on the MUB standard wells (wells 9-10). First, obtain the “homogenate blank” value by averaging the values in column 11.

steps.title: Then, take the MUB standard values,...
steps.instruction: Then, take the MUB standard values, average them for each MUB concentration, and subtract the homogenate blank from the fluorescence values for each concentration.

steps.title: Construct a standard curve using linear...
steps.instruction: Construct a standard curve using linear regression (MUB concentration on the x-axis, and Fluorescence on the y-axis) to obtain the slope. Make sure this is calculated with the MUB concentrations in μ mols/L (which is synonymous with nmols/mL).

steps.title: The slope is your Extinction Coefficient (ε )
steps.instruction: The slope is your Extinction Coefficient (ε ).

steps.title: To calculate the “quench”, simply construct...
steps.instruction: To calculate the “quench”, simply construct a second standard curve (in a separate plate) that receives buffer and no homogenate. This “unquenched” curve gives the true fluorescence of MUB.

steps.title: The ratio of the slope of...
steps.instruction: The ratio of the slope of the standard curve containing soils to the standard curve containing buffer tells you how much the soil quenches the fluorescence of the MUB.

steps.title: Activity (nmol h -1 g -1...
steps.instruction: Activity (nmol h -1 g -1 ) = {NFU x Buffer vol(mL)}/{ε x Homogenate vol(mL) x Time(h) x Soil mass(g)} NFU = net fluorescence units = {(Assay – Homogenate Control)/Quench Coeff} – Substrate control Quench coefficient = (Quench control – Homogenate control)/Standard Fluorescence ε = extinction coefficient Assay volume = 0.250 mL Homogenate volume = 0.200 mL Buffer vol = volume of buffer used to dilute the soil when making homogenate (likely 125 mL) Soil mass = mass of soil included when making homogenate (g) For soils, the mass of the soil can be wet weight, dry weight, organic matter, or Cmic.
